key_themes: AI disruption fears from competitors (Anthropic Claude Design, Canva, OpenAI)
Historically low valuation (~14x P/E, multi-year lows, high FCF yield)
Strong Firefly AI adoption, generative credit growth, and NVIDIA partnership
Record Q1 FY2026 results with double-digit ARR growth
CEO transition uncertainty amid otherwise solid fundamentals
summary: Adobe (ADBE) stock has sold off sharply to around $244 in mid-April 2026, hitting multi-year lows amid persistent AI disruption fears and the CEO succession announcement, despite delivering record Q1 FY2026 revenue of $6.4B (+12% YoY) and accelerating Firefly generative AI metrics.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ADBE/)[[2]](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/04/17/adobes-stock-hasnt-been-this-cheap-in-years-heres/)[[3]](https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/03/adobe-q1fy26-financial-results) Valuation is now at its cheapest levels in over a decade with strong free cash flow and 10%+ ARR guidance, prompting value investors (including Michael Burry trackers and AI model portfolios) to call it a compelling or generational buy with analyst targets averaging ~$330–$340 (35–40% upside). Sentiment is mixed in the short term due to headlines on tools like Claude Design pressuring the stock, but dominant investor themes favor the risk/reward as Adobe integrates AI to enhance rather than lose its deep creative software moat.
sources: 30+ web news/articles (Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool, Seeking Alpha, Forbes, Zacks, Barron's), 20+ recent X posts and investor discussions (semantic + keyword search)
